hero.subtitle: Organise, enable and reorder your mods per game on Windows, macOS, Linux and Steam Deck. Open source and free.

home.featuresSubtitle: Everything you need to keep your mods organised, in one place.
home.latestReleaseSubtitle: See what changed in the most recent version.
home.ctaTitle: Ready to tidy up your mods?
home.ctaSubtitle: Open source, verifiable and published through GitHub releases.

features.title: Features
features.subtitle: Everything Universal Mod Manager offers today, plus what's planned next.
features.ctaSubtitle: Grab the latest build and start managing your mods.

items.title: Cross-platform
items.description: A single, consistent app on Windows, macOS, Linux and Steam Deck.

items.title: Per-game mod lists
items.description: Manage a dedicated mod list for each game, kept neatly separate.

items.title: Enable & disable mods
items.description: Toggle any mod on or off without deleting it, so you can experiment freely.

items.title: Drag & drop ordering
items.description: Reorder your mods by dragging them to get the load order just right.

items.title: Plugin per game
items.description: Each game is supported by its own plugin, so support grows independently.

items.title: Themes
items.description: Make the interface yours with swappable themes.

items.title: Sandboxed plugins
items.description: Plugins run sandboxed: only predefined actions, no arbitrary code execution and no web requests.

items.title: Cloud sync
items.description: Planned: keep your mod setup in sync across all your devices.

install.title: Install
install.subtitle: Grab the latest build, then follow the steps for your platform. All downloads come from the official GitHub releases page.
install.olderVersions: Looking for older versions? Browse the

platforms.name: Windows
steps: Download the latest Windows installer (.exe) from the GitHub releases page.
Run the installer and follow the on-screen steps.
Launch Universal Mod Manager from the Start menu.

platforms.name: macOS
steps: Download the latest macOS package (.dmg) from the GitHub releases page.
Open the .dmg and drag the app into your Applications folder.
Launch it from Applications (allow it in System Settings on first run if prompted).

platforms.name: Linux
steps: Download the latest Linux build (AppImage) from the GitHub releases page.

platforms.name: Steam Deck
steps: Download the latest Linux build (AppImage) from the GitHub releases page.
Make it executable, run it, then add it to Steam as a non-Steam game for Gaming Mode.

security.title: Security
security.subtitle: Universal Mod Manager is built to be trustworthy. Here's how.

points.title: Open source
points.description: The full source code is public on GitHub. Anyone can read, audit and verify exactly what the app does. Nothing is hidden.

points.title: Verified downloads
points.description: Every build is published through official GitHub releases, so you always know a download comes from us and has not been tampered with.

points.title: Sandboxed plugins
points.description: Game support comes from plugins that run sandboxed: they can only perform a fixed set of predefined actions, with no arbitrary code execution and no web requests.
